session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: FakeLocalGitHub API Reference 3→read_when: 4→ - "writing tests that need fake GitHub PR or issue data" 5→ - "understanding FakeLocalGitHub.create_pr() auto-registration" 6→ - "using mutation tracking in LocalGitHub fake tests" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "creating a FakeLocalGitHub PR without checking auto-registration in _pr_details" 9→ warning: "FakeLocalGitHub.create_pr() auto-registers the PR in _pr_details. Manually adding to _pr_details after create_pr() causes duplicates." 10→ - action: "using context_for_test without matching parameter names to the current API" 11→ warning: "context_for_test() parameter names evolve. Check the current function signature before adding new parameters." 12→--- 13→ 14→# FakeLocalGitHub API Reference 15→ 16→Reference for the FakeLocalGitHub test double used throughout erk's test suite. Covers auto-registration behavior, mutation tracking, and common patterns. 17→ 18→## FakeLocalGitHub.create_pr() Auto-Registration 19→ 20→When `FakeLocalGitHub.create_pr()` is called, it automatically: 21→ 22→1. Creates the PR record 23→2. Registers it in `_pr_details` for subsequent `get_pr_details()` calls 24→3. Adds it to the open PRs list 25→ 26→Do NOT manually add to `_pr_details` after calling `create_pr()` — this causes duplicate entries. 27→ 28→## Mutation Tracking API 29→ 30→FakeLocalGitHub tracks all mutations for test assertions:
... [59 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Planless vs Planning Workflow Decision Framework 3→read_when: 4→ - "deciding whether to plan or start coding directly" 5→ - "realizing mid-task that the scope is larger than expected" 6→ - "choosing between erk wt create and plan mode" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "starting a multi-file change without entering plan mode" 9→ warning: "If the change touches 5+ files or has uncertain approach, plan first. See the decision matrix in when-to-switch-pattern.md." 10→ - action: "continuing to code after discovering scope is larger than expected" 11→ warning: "Stop and switch to planning. Mid-task warning signs (uncertainty accumulating, scope creeping, multiple valid approaches) indicate you should plan. See when-to-switch-pattern.md." 12→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 13→audit_result: edited 14→--- 15→ 16→# Planless vs Planning Workflow Decision Framework 17→ 18→Erk supports two workflows — planless (direct coding) and planning (plan-first). This document captures the cross-cutting decision framework for choosing between them and recognizing when to switch mid-task. 19→ 20→For the mechanics of each workflow, see the howto guides: [planless workflow](../../howto/planless-workflow.md) and [local workflow](../../howto/local-workflow.md). 21→ 22→For the mechanics of each workflow, see the planning workflow documentation in `docs/learned/planning/`. 23→ 24→## Why This Decision Matters 25→ 26→Choosing the wrong workflow has asymmetric costs: 27→ 28→- **Planning a trivial change** wastes minutes on overhead (issue creation, `.erk/impl-context/` setup, progress tracking). Low cost. 29→- **Skipping planning on a complex change** leads to scope creep, missed edge cases, poor architectural decisions, and potentially hours of wasted implementation. High cost. 30→
... [52 lines omitted] ...
45→## Mid-Task Warning Signs 71→**Switch too late:** Realizing deep into implementation that you should have planned means potentially discarding work. The 30-minute check and warning signs above catch this early.
session.assistant: Now I have all the files read. Let me start making the replacements. I'll process them file by file, handling the terminology changes as specified in the rules.

session.tool_result: <tool_use_error>String to replace not found in file. String: **using issue number from .erk/impl-context/plan-ref.json in a checkout footer** → Read [Checkout Footer Syntax](checkout-footer-syntax.md) first. session.tool_result: docs/learned/objectives/objective-lifecycle.md:437:3. **Objective**: extracted from plan issue metadata (`plan-header.objective_issue` field) docs/learned/objectives/roadmap-mutation-patterns.md:76:The full-body workflow uses a **bundled context fetch** (`erk exec objective-fetch-context`) to retrieve the objective issue, plan issue, and PR details in a single CLI call. This exists because the command needs all three to compose the update, and fetching them in separate LLM turns would waste tokens and add latency. docs/learned/objectives/objective-roadmap-check.md:42:| `erk-objective` label | Prevents running check on plan issues or random issues that happen to contain markdown tables | docs/learned/objectives/research-documentation-integration.md:48:The learn workflow creates a separate plan issue for documentation that goes through human review before implementation — insights are captured but not committed without review. See [Learn Workflow](../planning/learn-workflow.md) for the full pipeline.

docs/learned/tui/data-contract.md:161:| `learn_plan_issue_closed` | `bool` | Yes | Whether the learn plan issue is closed | docs/learned/tui/plan-row-data.md:95:| `learn_plan_issue_closed` | `bool \| None` | Whether the learn plan issue is closed | Yes |

docs/learned/pr-operations/plan-embedding-in-pr.md:18:When a PR originates from an erk-plan issue, reviewers need the original plan for context — it explains the _intent_ behind the code, not just the diff. Embedding the plan directly in the PR body means reviewers don't have to chase cross-references to a separate GitHub issue. A collapsible `<details>` section keeps this context available without overwhelming the PR description. docs/learned/pr-operations/plan-embedding-in-pr.md:25:2. **GitHub API** → fetches the plan issue body, then the plan comment containing the actual plan markdown docs/learned/pr-operations/pr-validation-rules.md:9: warning: "Checkout footers require the PR number (from create_pr return value), NOT the plan issue number. See pr-validation-rules.md." docs/learned/pr-operations/pr-submit-phases.md:60:The `PlanContextProvider` checks for a linked erk-plan issue: docs/learned/pr-operations/checkout-footer-syntax.md:36:The single most common agent mistake is using the plan issue number from `.erk/impl-context/plan-ref.json` instead of the PR number in the checkout command. These are different GitHub entities: docs/learned/pr-operations/tripwires.md:41:**using issue number from .erk/impl-context/plan-ref.json in a checkout footer** → Read [PR Validation Rules](pr-validation-rules.md) first. Checkout footers require the PR number (from create_pr return value), NOT the plan issue number. See pr-validation-rules.md.

session.assistant: Let me search for any remaining instances to check in other files:
session.tool_result: docs/learned/ci/learn-ci-environment-detection.md:55:**Why skip confirmations entirely?** The learn workflow was designed to be safe to run automatically — it creates a plan issue but doesn't merge code or modify production state. Auto-proceeding in CI reduces friction without introducing risk. docs/learned/reference/gastown-analysis.md:710:- Add severity field to plan issues docs/learned/cli/pr-rewrite.md:30:4. **Generate AI title/body** — Uses `CommitMessageGenerator` with optional plan context from linked erk-plan issue docs/learned/tui/data-contract.md:161:| `learn_plan_issue_closed` | `bool` | Yes | Whether the learn plan issue is closed | docs/learned/tui/plan-row-data.md:95:| `learn_plan_issue_closed` | `bool \| None` | Whether the learn plan issue is closed | Yes | docs/learned/documentation/when-to-switch-pattern.md:63:**Switching from planning to planless (rare):** If a plan reveals the task is simpler than expected, close the plan issue and make the change directly. This is uncommon — if you invested in planning, the implementation usually benefits from the plan's structure. docs/learned/cli/slash-command-llm-turn-optimization.md:47:The `system:objective-update-with-landed-pr` command demonstrates this: Step 1 calls `erk exec objective-fetch-context` once to fetch the objective issue, plan issue, PR details, and parsed roadmap context. The command then has everything it needs to compose the update and post the comment in parallel — reducing ~8 turns to ~4. docs/learned/cli/workflow-commands.md:20: PR workflows automatically update plan issue dispatch metadata when the docs/learned/cli/commands/pr-summarize.md:57:1. **Plan context** - From linked erk-plan issue (highest priority) docs/learned/cli/commands/pr-summarize.md:73:When a branch is associated with a plan, the command automatically fetches plan context from the linked erk-plan issue. docs/learned/workflows/one-shot-workflow.md:10: warning: "Branch format is `oneshot-{slug}-{MM-DD-HHMM}` (no plan issue) or `P{N}-{slug}-{MM-DD-HHMM}` (when plan_issue_number is provided). The workflow and CLI both depend on these prefixes for identification." docs/learned/workflows/tripwires.md:21:**modifying one-shot branch naming convention** → Read [One-Shot Workflow](one-shot-workflow.md) first. Branch format is `oneshot-{slug}-{MM-DD-HHMM}` (no plan issue) or `P{N}-{slug}-{MM-DD-HHMM}` (when plan_issue_number is provided). The workflow and CLI both depend on these prefixes for identification. docs/learned/erk/pr-commands.md:10: warning: "Checkout footer requires the PR number (from gh pr create output), NOT the plan issue number from .erk/impl-context/plan-ref.json."
session.assistant: Let me fix the remaining instances:
